criteria.label: Write the model ŷ = Xw + b and define each symbol (X, w, b, y).
criteria.example: On paper: X is n×d design matrix, w is d×1 weights, b is intercept (or absorbed in ones column), y is n×1 continuous targets.

criteria.label: State the OLS objective J(w) in summation and matrix form.
criteria.example: Write J(w) = (1/n)Σ(yᵢ − ŷᵢ)² = (1/n)‖y − Xw‖² and identify it as MSE.

criteria.label: Derive or recall the normal equation and when it applies.
criteria.example: Set ∇_w J = 0 → XᵀXw = Xᵀy → w* = (XᵀX)⁻¹Xᵀy when X has full column rank.

criteria.label: Explain the projection intuition for OLS geometrically.
criteria.example: Sketch y in Rⁿ, span of X columns, orthogonal residual y − ŷ ⊥ each column of X.

criteria.label: Implement or use code to fit linear regression and report validation RMSE.
criteria.example: sklearn LinearRegression on train split; print test RMSE = sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, y_pred)).

criteria.label: Identify when to use gradient descent instead of the normal equation.
criteria.example: Name two cases: large d where O(d³) inversion is costly, or collinear X where iterative regularized methods are safer.
